title: Make hierarchy reflect the job
statement: Sibling surfaces with different jobs need visibly different hierarchy; sections should not merely look equally flat.
rationale: A consistent component system still needs enough hierarchy to communicate role and importance.

prefer: Use hierarchy to distinguish primary work, supporting context, and metadata.
Keep sibling styling consistent without erasing role differences.
avoid: Giving every section the same weight because they share a component family.

when: Adjacent sections serve different user jobs or importance levels.
not_when: The sections are true peers that users compare directly.

evidence.summary: Asked visually sibling action sections to use the same hierarchy and questioned an unnecessary undocked footer boundary.
Rejected flat Includes and Activity sections and asked for clearer organization.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Use hierarchy to distinguish primary work, supporting context, and metadata.
Keep sibling styling consistent without erasing role differences.
negative_examples: Giving every section the same weight because they share a component family.

checks: Can a skimmer tell which section owns the primary job?
Are peers equal and non-peers differentiated?
Does hierarchy remain within the shared system?
